steps.instructions: Determine whether the warmer was recently operating at elevated heat output or after extended use. Confirm that nothing was placed over or near the heater head that could trap heat or restrict ventilation. Allow the heater head to cool before retesting.

steps.title: Confirm Power and External Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord, use a known-good outlet according to facility procedures, and check for liquid intrusion, unusual odor, heat damage, discoloration, contamination, or visible damage near the heater head or base.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Error Has Cleared
steps.instructions: After confirming airflow openings are clear and the heater head has cooled, operate the warmer only in a controlled test setting. If the error does not return, complete required functional and safety checks before returning it to service. If the error returns, remove the warmer from service and route it for authorized bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 06 [0B XX 00 00] during warming operation, and radiant heat was unavailable or considered unreliable for patient use.
CCR.Cause: Heater duct and heater head vents were inspected for obstruction. Restricted airflow was identified as a possible overheating cause, or the error returned after cooling with airflow openings clear, consistent with a persistent heater safety-circuit fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed warmer from patient use and inspected heater airflow openings. Cleared external obstruction and completed required verification when the error resolved, or labeled the unit Out of Service and routed it for authorized bench evaluation when the error persisted.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 09 [0B 00 00 00] Thermostat Open During Run Time
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 09 [0B 00 00 00] when an open thermostat condition interrupts radiant heat operation and prevents reliable infant warming.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the Panda Warmer from patient use if Sys Fail 09 [0B 00 00 00] occurs during warming or preparation for use. Transfer the infant to another approved warming method or a known-working warmer before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm that the display shows Sys Fail 09 [0B 00 00 00]. Document whether the error occurs at startup, after operating, after extended heating operation, or intermittently, and note any loss of heat output or repeated shutdown.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Basic External Condition
steps.instructions: Confirm use of an approved hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the power cord, plug, strain relief, and heater head exterior for damage, looseness, odor, discoloration, overheating, or other visible abnormalities.

steps.title: Inspect the Heater Duct and Heater Head Vents for Blockage
steps.instructions: Allow the heater head to cool. Inspect airflow paths and vents for dust, lint, drapes, blankets, covers, stored items, tape, labels, debris, contamination, or visible damage. Remove external obstructions according to facility procedure.

steps.title: Determine Whether Vent Blockage Caused an Over-Temperature Condition
steps.instructions: If a blockage is found, document and correct it. After the heater head has cooled, perform an out-of-service operational check and monitor for recurrence of Sys Fail 09 [0B 00 00 00].

steps.title: Evaluate a Persistent Thermostat-Related Failure
steps.instructions: If no external blockage is found or the error returns after correction and cooling, keep the warmer out of clinical use and route it for qualified bench evaluation of the thermostat and heater-control safety circuit.

steps.title: Isolate a Persistent Device-Related Failure
steps.instructions: If Sys Fail 09 [0B 00 00 00] persists after external causes are ruled out, remove the warmer from service, label it Out of Service, and escalate for repair or bench evaluation, including Power PCA replacement when indicated by authorized service findings.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 09 [0B 00 00 00] during operation and stopped providing reliable controlled heat.
CCR.Cause: Inspection identified blocked heater head ventilation consistent with an over-temperature thermostat opening condition. The obstruction was cleared and the heater head was allowed to cool.
CCR.Resolution: Removed warmer from patient use, cleared obstruction from heater head vents, completed out-of-service functional verification without recurrence of Sys Fail 09 [0B 00 00 00], and returned the unit to service according to departmental policy.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 09 [XX 80 XX XX] Heater Control Failure
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 09 [XX 80 XX XX] when heater control fails during operation, interrupting reliable infant warming capability and requiring removal from clinical use.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the Panda Warmer from patient use if Sys Fail 09 [XX 80 XX XX] occurs during warming operation. Transfer the infant to an approved alternate warming method or another functioning warmer according to clinical procedure. Do not rely on a warmer with an unresolved heater control failure.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: With the warmer removed from patient use, confirm that the display reports Sys Fail 09 [XX 80 XX XX]. Document whether the error occurs during startup, shortly after power-on, during active warming operation, or intermittently after operation. Note interrupted heating, system failure indication, loss of normal operation, or inability to maintain warming readiness.

steps.title: Remove the Unit From Clinical Service Before Further Evaluation
steps.instructions: Disconnect the warmer from patient-care use and attach an appropriate Out of Service label while troubleshooting is performed. Do not attempt functional warming checks with an infant under the heater.

steps.title: Inspect the External Condition of the Warmer
steps.instructions: Inspect for liquid intrusion, contamination, damaged heater-head components, burn odor, discoloration, unusual heat, evidence of overheating, blocked ventilation openings, and damage to the power cord, plug, or strain relief.

steps.title: Verify Power Source and Basic Power Condition
steps.instructions: Confirm the warmer is connected to an approved hospital-grade outlet and that the power cord and plug are intact. When appropriate, test the outlet using approved Clinical Engineering procedures or connect the warmer to a known-good approved power source for observation.

steps.title: Confirm the Failure Is Not Due to an Obvious Setup or Environmental Condition
steps.instructions: Ensure there are no external obstructions, contamination concerns, unusual environmental conditions, operator-selected settings, interrupted use conditions, or obvious external physical problems that explain the failure.

steps.title: Isolate the Condition as a Device-Related Heater Control Failure
steps.instructions: If the error remains confirmed after external inspection and basic power verification, classify the failure as device-related. For Sys Fail 09 [XX 80 XX XX], the reported service correction is replacement of the Power PCA. Keep the warmer removed from service and route it for qualified repair or bench evaluation.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: After approved repair, complete all required functional, safety, and performance verification before returning the warmer to service. If repair has not been completed, the warmer must remain out of service.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 09 [XX 80 XX XX] Heater Control Failure during operation, with reliable radiant warming function no longer assured.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed Sys Fail 09 [XX 80 XX XX] heater control failure indication during run time. External inspection, ventilation review, and basic power-source checks did not identify an external cause. Error condition indicates internal Power PCA failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed warmer from patient use, labeled unit Out of Service, documented confirmed error condition, and routed device for qualified repair or bench evaluation with Power PCA replacement required before return to service.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 09 [0A XX 00 00] Heater Safety Relay Open During Operation
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 09 [0A XX 00 00] when an open Heater Safety Relay interrupts radiant warmer heat control during operation and may prevent reliable infant warming.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the Panda Warmer from patient use if Sys Fail 09 [0A XX 00 00] occurs during warming or preparation for use. Notify clinical staff and transfer the infant to another approved warming device or clinical environment according to facility procedure. Do not rely on the warmer for thermal support while the heater-control failure remains unresolved.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: With the warmer removed from patient use, confirm that the display reports Sys Fail 09 [0A XX 00 00]. Document when the error occurs and observe for loss of radiant heat output, inability to enter normal warming operation, repeated system failure messages, audible alarms, or interrupted operation.

steps.title: Inspect the Warmer for Obvious External Concerns
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the heater head, control area, power cord, plug, and accessible cabling for physical damage, contamination, fluid intrusion, discoloration, unusual odor, overheating, or strained cabling. Do not continue powered troubleshooting if an unsafe physical condition is present.

steps.title: Confirm Power Source and Basic Operation
steps.instructions: Verify the warmer is connected to a known-good, properly grounded outlet and inspect the power cord and plug for damage. With the unit out of patient use, determine whether the same system failure returns during startup or when heater operation is initiated.

steps.title: Review Accessible Connection Concerns
steps.instructions: Inspect externally accessible cabling and connections for looseness, damage, contamination, or strain. Document any recent movement, impact, service, or contamination. Do not perform internal disassembly during frontline troubleshooting.

steps.title: Retest After Correcting Any External Issue
steps.instructions: After correcting any external power, cord, or accessible connection concern, power the warmer on while it remains out of patient use. Observe whether Sys Fail 09 [0A XX 00 00] returns. If the error clears, complete required functional verification and electrical safety testing before considering return to service.

steps.title: Escalate Persistent Heater-Control Failure
steps.instructions: If the error remains after external inspection and power verification, remove the warmer from service for bench evaluation. Provide the exact error, observed behavior, and completed external checks. Service information indicates evaluation of the heater-control pathway, including the Power PCA-to-Control PCA connection and indicated PCA replacement if confirmed during bench repair.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Confirm the error no longer recurs, verify normal warming function, complete required safety and performance checks, document findings and corrective action, and return the warmer to service only after it passes all required checks.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 09 [0A XX 00 00] during operation. Radiant heat function was interrupted and the unit was removed from patient use.
CCR.Cause: Verified Sys Fail 09 [0A XX 00 00]. Power source and external device condition were acceptable. No accessible external damage or connection issue was identified. Persistent error indicates heater safety-relay or heater-control pathway failure requiring bench evaluation.
CCR.Resolution: Removed warmer from clinical use and labeled Out of Service. Routed unit for bench repair evaluation of the heater-control pathway and required post-repair functional and electrical safety testing before return to service.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 11 [AA AA AA AA] System Failure Communication Error
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 11 [AA AA AA AA] when processor communication failure prevents reliable infant warmer heat-control operation and requires removal from clinical use until verified safe.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the warmer is supporting an infant or prepared for immediate clinical use, notify clinical staff and transfer the patient to a known-working warming device before troubleshooting. Do not rely on the affected warmer for thermal support until the error is resolved and safe operation is verified.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: With the warmer removed from patient use, confirm that the display reports Sys Fail 11 [AA AA AA AA]. Document whether it occurs immediately at startup, after partial startup, after recent use, or approximately one minute after a power-on attempt.

steps.title: Inspect the Warmer for External Heat or Ventilation Concerns
steps.instructions: Inspect heater head cooling vents for obstruction, dust buildup, linens, covers, or nearby equipment restricting airflow. Remove visible obstructions and note whether the heater head remains unusually warm from recent use.

steps.title: Confirm the Incoming Mains Power Is Correct
steps.instructions: Verify the warmer is connected to the correct facility mains voltage for its configuration. Inspect the power cord and plug for visible damage or overheating and verify the outlet or approved power source according to facility procedure.

steps.title: Reattempt Startup After External Conditions Are Corrected
steps.instructions: After verifying correct mains voltage and clear heater head vents, perform a controlled power-up with the warmer removed from clinical use. If the error clears, complete required functional and safety verification before return to service.

steps.title: Check for Related Overtemperature Error History When Applicable
steps.instructions: If the warmer was recently operating and the error appears approximately one minute after a power-on attempt, authorized Clinical Engineering personnel should review Service Mode error history for Sys Fail 09 0B or Sys Fail 06.

steps.title: Refer for Qualified Bench Evaluation of the Power PCA Fuse Condition
steps.instructions: If the failure persists after external checks, remove the warmer from service for qualified bench evaluation. Authorized service personnel should evaluate Power PCA fuses F9 and F10 according to approved procedure. Loose fuse retention requires Power PCA replacement and should not be corrected by bending the fuse clips.

steps.title: Confirm Resolution Before Returning the Warmer to Service
steps.instructions: After approved correction or repair, confirm that Sys Fail 11 [AA AA AA AA] no longer appears. Complete operational verification, alarm checks, heater-function testing, and electrical safety testing before documenting return to service.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displays Sys Fail 11 [AA AA AA AA] during startup. Warmer does not complete normal operation and cannot be relied upon for infant heat support.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed Sys Fail 11 [AA AA AA AA] processor communication failure during startup. Incoming mains voltage was verified, heater head vents were inspected, and related overtemperature error history was reviewed. Persistent failure requires qualified evaluation of the Power PCA, fuse condition, thermostat circuit, or processor communication path.
CCR.Resolution: Removed warmer from patient use and labeled Out of Service. Verified power source and heater head ventilation condition, reviewed applicable error history, and referred unit for qualified bench repair evaluation. Device will not be returned to service until operational and safety verification is successfully completed.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 11 [XX 00 00 00] or [FF FF FF FF] Heat Control Processor Communication Failure
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 11 when communication between the heat-control and main application processors is lost, making infant warmer heat-control operation unreliable or unavailable.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Transfer the infant to a known-working warming device before troubleshooting and remove the Panda Warmer from patient use while Sys Fail 11 remains unresolved.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the display shows Sys Fail 11 [XX 00 00 00] or Sys Fail 11 [FF FF FF FF], document when it occurs, and record the XX value when present.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Basic External Conditions
steps.instructions: Confirm use of a known-good hospital-grade outlet, inspect the power cord and plug, and look for damage, overheating, discoloration, fluid contamination, or signs of impact.

steps.title: Confirm the Failure Is Not Cleared by an Approved Power Reset
steps.instructions: With the warmer removed from patient use, perform an approved shutdown and restart, then observe whether Sys Fail 11 clears, reappears immediately, or returns intermittently.

steps.title: Inspect Accessible External Control-System Indicators and Condition
steps.instructions: Without opening the device, inspect for blank or frozen display behavior, unresponsive controls, unexpected heater indications, loose external panels, visible damage, moisture, or contamination.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Error Is Persistent
steps.instructions: If the error remains after external power, restart, and visual-condition checks, classify the problem as a persistent internal communication failure involving the Heat Control Processor and Main Application Processor.

steps.title: Escalate for Controlled Bench Evaluation
steps.instructions: Remove the warmer from service and route it for authorized bench evaluation of the ribbon cable between the Power PCA and Control PCA, the Control PCA, and the Power PCA supply path according to service documentation.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Return the warmer to clinical use only after the identified corrective action is completed and required operational and safety verification confirms reliable heater-control operation.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 11 [XX 00 00 00], indicating loss of communication between the Heat Control Processor and Main Application Processor. Warmer removed from patient use due to unresolved heat-control reliability concern.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed recurring Sys Fail 11 after testing with a known-good outlet and completing an approved restart. No external damage, power-cord defect, or contamination identified. Fault is consistent with an internal processor communication, ribbon-cable, Control PCA, or Power PCA supply-path failure requiring bench evaluation.
CCR.Resolution: Removed Panda Warmer from service, applied Out of Service labeling, and routed unit for authorized internal inspection and repair. Device will not be returned to clinical use until the error is corrected and required operational and safety verification is completed.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 02 Heater Power Feedback Mismatch
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 02 when heater power feedback does not match commanded output, creating unreliable warming operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the warmer from patient use. If an infant is being supported, notify clinical staff and transfer the infant to a known-working warming device before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Sys Fail 02 is displayed and document whether it occurs at startup, when heater output is commanded, during warm-up, or intermittently during operation.

steps.title: Inspect External Condition and Basic Setup
steps.instructions: Inspect the heater assembly, power cord, plug, exterior surfaces, heat engine area, and power source for visible damage, contamination, obstructions, overheating evidence, or unsafe conditions.

steps.title: Confirm the Error Is Not Cleared by a Safe Power Restart
steps.instructions: With the device removed from patient use and no unsafe physical condition present, perform a normal power restart and determine whether Sys Fail 02 returns.

steps.title: Run the Heat Engine Health Check in Service Mode
steps.instructions: Following approved Clinical Engineering service procedures, run the Heat Engine Health Check and document whether the test passes, fails, or causes Sys Fail 02 to recur.

steps.title: Check Accessible Connections Between the Control PCA and Power PCA
steps.instructions: With the warmer appropriately powered down, inspect accessible Control PCA-to-Power PCA connections for looseness, damage, overheating evidence, harness strain, or contamination. Reseat only according to approved service practices and retest.

steps.title: Check Connections Between the Power PCA and the Heat Engine
steps.instructions: Inspect accessible Power PCA-to-heat engine connections for looseness, damage, discoloration, contamination, or overheating evidence, then repeat the Heat Engine Health Check if a safely addressable issue is corrected.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Has Been Resolved
steps.instructions: Return the warmer to clinical service only after the Heat Engine Health Check passes, Sys Fail 02 does not return, and required return-to-service verification confirms normal heater operation.

CCR.Complaint: Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 02 during operation. Reported heater power did not match commanded heater power, making warming performance unreliable.
CCR.Cause: Heat Engine Health Check failed and Sys Fail 02 persisted after verifying the power source and inspecting accessible connections between the Control PCA, Power PCA, and heat engine. Findings indicate likely Power PCA failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed warmer from clinical service, labeled unit Out of Service, documented failed heater power verification, and routed unit for bench repair and Power PCA evaluation/replacement.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 05 ADC Configuration Error or ADC Failure
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 05 when an ADC configuration or reference-channel failure prevents confidence in accurate sensing and reliable warmer control operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the Panda Warmer from patient use if Sys Fail 05 occurs while supporting an infant or during preparation for care. Notify clinical staff and transfer the infant to a known-working warming device before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: With the warmer removed from patient use, power the unit on and confirm whether Sys Fail 05 is displayed. Document whether the error occurs during startup, self-test, warming operation, or intermittently, along with any abnormal control or heater behavior.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Basic External Conditions
steps.instructions: Confirm connection to a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the power cord, plug, strain relief, and visible power-entry area for damage, looseness, overheating, or contamination.

steps.title: Inspect the Unit for Visible Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the control panel, display, warmer head, column, connectors, and accessible external surfaces for fluid intrusion, impact damage, burn marks, unusual odor, loose connectors, or overheating. Stop testing if unsafe conditions are identified.

steps.title: Confirm That External Accessories Are Not Contributing
steps.instructions: Inspect and disconnect removable external accessories not required to observe the startup fault, following facility procedure. Check accessory connectors and cables for bent pins, contamination, damage, or improper connection, then observe whether Sys Fail 05 remains.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Error Persists After Basic Checks
steps.instructions: After confirming safe power, visible condition, and external accessory status, power the warmer on again while it remains out of patient use. If Sys Fail 05 returns or remains active, stop operational troubleshooting and route the unit for bench evaluation.

steps.title: Escalate for Bench Evaluation or Repair
steps.instructions: Persistent Sys Fail 05 indicates likely internal ADC/reference-channel or Control PCA failure. Qualified service personnel should follow approved procedures to reset NVRAM defaults and replace the Control PCA if the error persists, followed by required verification and safety testing.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 05 during startup and could not be considered reliable for patient warming.
CCR.Cause: Verified stable outlet power and inspected external condition and removable accessory connections. Sys Fail 05 remained reproducible, indicating likely internal ADC/reference-channel or Control PCA failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed warmer from service and labeled Out of Service. Routed unit for qualified bench evaluation, including manufacturer-directed NVRAM default reset and Control PCA replacement if the error persists. Return to service pending successful repair verification and safety testing.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 08 [XX XX 00 00] Shorted or Incorrectly Detected Touch-Panel Button
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 08 [XX XX 00 00] when a shorted or incorrectly detected touch-panel button prevents reliable warmer control operation and may require Touch Panel or Control PCA repair.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Panda Warmer is supporting an infant or prepared for immediate clinical use, notify clinical staff and transfer the patient to a known-working warming device before troubleshooting. Do not rely on a warmer with an unresolved touch-panel or control-interface system failure.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: With the warmer removed from patient use, power the unit on and confirm Sys Fail 08 [XX XX 00 00]. Document whether it occurs during startup, Service Mode entry, after a button is touched, or intermittently, and note any stuck or unresponsive controls.

steps.title: Rule Out Incorrect Service Mode Entry
steps.instructions: If the error occurred while attempting to enter Service Mode, confirm that both required Service Mode buttons are depressed during power-on according to the manufacturer procedure. If correct entry clears the error, document the condition and verify normal operation.

steps.title: Inspect the Touch Panel Externally
steps.instructions: Inspect for stuck buttons, damaged overlay material, residue, liquid intrusion, cleaning-product buildup, contamination, or objects pressing against the control panel. Correct or clean only according to approved guidance.

steps.title: Use the Button-Function Diagnostic Test
steps.instructions: If Service Mode can be entered properly, use the approved button-function diagnostic test to determine whether one or more buttons are continuously detected as active without intentional pressing.

steps.title: Check the Touch-Panel Connection
steps.instructions: With the warmer removed from service and following approved electrical safety precautions, inspect the touch-panel connection to the Control PCA for looseness, partial seating, visible damage, contamination, or evidence of liquid exposure. Reseat only when permitted, then repower and retest.

steps.title: Isolate the Touch Panel From the Control Path
steps.instructions: Qualified service personnel should follow the approved service procedure to disconnect the touch-panel connector from the Control PCA and repower the unit. If Sys Fail 08 clears, the Touch Panel is the likely failed component. If it remains, the Control PCA is the likely failed component.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved and Verify Function
steps.instructions: After correction or authorized component replacement, confirm Sys Fail 08 no longer appears during normal startup, touch-panel buttons respond correctly, no inputs remain falsely detected, and required operational and safety checks are completed before returning the warmer to service.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 08 [XX XX 00 00] during startup. Touch-panel button input appeared continuously detected, preventing confidence in normal control-panel operation.
CCR.Cause: Verified correct Service Mode button entry. External obstruction and contamination were not identified. Button-function diagnostics identified a continuously detected touch-panel input. Sys Fail 08 cleared when the touch-panel connector was disconnected from the Control PCA, indicating a failed Touch Panel.
CCR.Resolution: Removed warmer from clinical service, labeled unit Out of Service, and routed for Touch Panel replacement and post-repair operational verification before return to patient use.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 01 [FF XX XX XX] Flash Memory or File System Error
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 01 [FF XX XX XX] when a flash memory or file system error prevents reliable warmer startup, control, or safe clinical operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Panda Warmer is occupied or needed for immediate patient care, notify clinical staff and transfer the infant to a known-working warming device according to clinical procedure. Do not rely on an infant warmer displaying an unresolved Sys Fail 01 [FF XX XX XX] error for temperature support or monitoring.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: With the unit removed from patient use, power the warmer on and confirm the exact displayed error: Sys Fail 01 [FF XX XX XX]. Document when the error appears, whether startup completes, whether the error is constant or intermittent, and whether display, controls, heater operation, or alarms appear abnormal.

steps.title: Check the Power Source and Basic External Conditions
steps.instructions: Confirm that the warmer is connected to an approved, functioning outlet and that the power cord is fully inserted and free of visible damage. When permitted by facility procedure, test the outlet or use a known-working outlet. Inspect for liquid intrusion, contamination, burnt odor, abnormal heat, visible damage, impact, or transport damage.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Power Restart
steps.instructions: After confirming the warmer is not supporting a patient, power the unit off normally, disconnect it from AC power briefly, reconnect it to a verified outlet, and power it back on. Observe whether Sys Fail 01 [FF XX XX XX] returns during startup. If the error clears, complete functional verification according to facility procedure before return to service.

steps.title: Confirm That the Problem Is Not Accessory-Related
steps.instructions: Check for attached external accessories or optional connections. Disconnect only nonessential external accessories as allowed by facility procedure, then restart the warmer again. Do not perform internal disassembly during external troubleshooting.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Error Is Persistent
steps.instructions: If Sys Fail 01 [FF XX XX XX] repeatedly returns after verified power, external inspection, controlled restart, and removal of nonessential external connections, treat the condition as a persistent internal system failure. Document the exact displayed values, timing, and troubleshooting findings.

steps.title: Escalate for Qualified Bench Evaluation
steps.instructions: Remove the warmer from service and route it for qualified repair evaluation. Manufacturer-directed repair action may include reloading applicable software, reprogramming the main application flash, or replacing the Control PCA if the error persists after approved restoration procedures. Return the unit to clinical use only after successful verification.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 01 [FF XX XX XX] during startup and did not complete normal readiness for clinical use.
CCR.Cause: Verified stable external power, intact power cord, no visible contamination or damage, and no accessory-related cause. Error returned after controlled restart, indicating likely internal flash memory, file system, software, or Control PCA failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed Panda Warmer from service, labeled unit Out of Service, and routed for qualified bench evaluation for approved software reload or main application flash reprogramming, with Control PCA replacement if the error persists.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 03 Memory Error Occurred
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 03 when a memory error prevents reliable warmer startup, control operation, settings retention, or safe infant warming use.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the Panda Warmer from patient use if Sys Fail 03 is displayed during setup or while supporting an infant. Transfer the patient to a known-working warming device according to clinical procedure before troubleshooting begins.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: With the unit removed from patient use, confirm that the display shows Sys Fail 03. Document when the error occurs and whether startup failure, unavailable controls, repeated system-failure messages, settings retention issues, alarms, or interrupted heater operation are present.

steps.title: Check Power Source and External Conditions
steps.instructions: Verify the warmer is connected to a known-good, properly grounded outlet. Inspect the power cord, plug, strain relief, and receptacle for damage, looseness, contamination, or overheating. Power-cycle the unit once using the approved startup procedure while it remains off patient use.

steps.title: Inspect the Warmer for External Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect for impact damage, fluid intrusion, heavy contamination, loose external panels, unusual odor, or evidence of overheating. Determine whether the unit was recently cleaned, moved, dropped, exposed to fluid, or affected by a power interruption.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Error Clears or Returns
steps.instructions: Restart the warmer after external checks are complete. Confirm whether Sys Fail 03 clears and normal startup completes, or whether the error returns, recurs intermittently, or prevents normal operation. If it clears, complete required operational, electrical safety, and performance verification before return to service.

steps.title: Isolate the Condition as Device-Related
steps.instructions: If stable power and acceptable external condition have been verified but Sys Fail 03 remains present, treat the issue as an internal memory or control-system failure requiring qualified bench evaluation.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Return the warmer to service only after the error clears and the unit successfully passes required operational verification, electrical safety checks, and performance testing according to facility procedure.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 03 during startup. Unit could not be confirmed reliable for infant warming and was removed from clinical use.
CCR.Cause: Verified known-good outlet and inspected external power components and warmer condition with no external issue identified. Sys Fail 03 returned after restart, indicating likely internal memory, NVRAM retention, application flash, or Control PCA-related failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed warmer from service and labeled Out of Service for qualified bench evaluation and repair. Unit not returned to clinical use pending correction of the memory fault and successful post-repair operational and safety verification.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 04 Memory Redundancy Error
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 04 Memory Redundancy Error, indicating mismatched redundantly stored variables that may prevent reliable warmer control, alarm operation, or safe clinical use.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the Panda Warmer from patient use when Sys Fail 04 is displayed. If an infant is under the warmer, notify clinical staff and transfer warming support to a known-working device before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm that the display reports Sys Fail 04 and document whether it occurs at power-up, during self-test, after operation, or intermittently after power cycling. Note any startup, control, heating, or alarm abnormalities.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Basic External Conditions
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord, plug, and device inlet for damage, looseness, contamination, or overheating. Connect the warmer to a known-good grounded hospital-grade outlet and observe startup behavior.

steps.title: Inspect for External Damage or Environmental Concerns
steps.instructions: Inspect for fluid intrusion, contamination, impact damage, unusual odor, overheating, or damage to the display and controls. Keep the unit out of service if unsafe external conditions are identified.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Power Restart
steps.instructions: With the unit out of patient use, power it off fully and restart it using the verified outlet. Observe whether Sys Fail 04 returns during startup or self-test.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Fault Is Persistent
steps.instructions: If Sys Fail 04 returns after external inspection, verified power, and controlled restart, classify it as a persistent internal Memory Redundancy Error involving mismatched redundantly stored variables.

steps.title: Escalate for Authorized Service Action
steps.instructions: Remove the warmer from clinical use and refer it for authorized bench evaluation or manufacturer-directed service. The identified corrective action is reprogramming the main application flash; if the problem persists, Control PCA replacement is indicated.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: After authorized corrective action, complete required operational verification, alarm checks, and applicable electrical safety testing. Return the warmer to service only after reliable operation is confirmed and documented.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 04 Memory Redundancy Error during startup. Unit could not be confirmed reliable for patient warming and was removed from clinical use.
CCR.Cause: Verified stable outlet power and inspected external condition with no correctable external issue found. Sys Fail 04 returned after controlled restart, consistent with a persistent internal memory redundancy failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for authorized bench evaluation and approved reprogramming of the main application flash; Control PCA replacement or vendor repair required if the error remains.

title: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer Infant Warmer - Error: Sys Fail 10 Self-Calibration Failure
description: Troubleshooting Sys Fail 10 [XX XX XX XX] when self-calibration fails, preventing reliable infant warmer operation and requiring removal from clinical use.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately remove the Panda Warmer from patient use if Sys Fail 10 occurs while the warmer is occupied, prepared for use, or needed for patient care. Transfer the infant to a known-working warming device according to clinical procedure before performing troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: With the warmer removed from patient use, confirm that the display shows Sys Fail 10 [XX XX XX XX]. Document the displayed hexadecimal values exactly as shown and determine when the error occurs.

steps.title: Check the Power Source and External Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord, plug, strain relief, and power-entry area for visible damage, looseness, contamination, overheating, or liquid intrusion. Connect the warmer to a verified hospital-grade outlet for evaluation only after removal from patient use.

steps.title: Inspect the Unit for External Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Check for fluid intrusion, impact damage, cracked housing, loose assemblies, burnt odor, unusual heat, abnormal sounds, or control-panel damage. Do not open the unit during initial troubleshooting.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Power Restart
steps.instructions: With the unit out of service and disconnected from patient care, power the warmer off, wait briefly, and power it back on using a verified outlet. Observe whether Sys Fail 10 [XX XX XX XX] returns during startup.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Failure Requires Repair Evaluation
steps.instructions: If external conditions are ruled out and Sys Fail 10 returns, classify the issue as a device-related failure requiring qualified repair or bench evaluation. Manufacturer corrective information identifies Control PCA replacement as the indicated repair action.

steps.title: Confirm Safe Disposition
steps.instructions: Do not return the warmer to patient use based solely on one successful restart. Complete required functional inspection, alarm verification, and safety testing before return to service, or remove the unit from service and escalate for repair if the error remains or recurs.

CCR.Complaint: GE Healthcare Panda Warmer displayed Sys Fail 10 [XX XX XX XX] during startup, indicating self-calibration failure. Unit was not considered safe for patient use.
CCR.Cause: Verified hospital-grade power source and inspected external condition with no correctable external issue identified. Sys Fail 10 returned after controlled restart, indicating likely internal Control PCA failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed Panda Warmer from service, labeled unit Out of Service, documented the displayed fault values, and routed the device for qualified bench repair and Control PCA evaluation/replacement as indicated.
